text:
(a) The department may issue a commercial driver’s license with endorsements:

(1) authorizing the driving of a vehicle transporting hazardous materials, subject to the requirements of Title 49 C.F.R. Part 1572;

(2) authorizing the towing of a double or triple trailer or a trailer over a specified weight;

(3) authorizing the driving of a vehicle carrying passengers;

(4) authorizing the driving of a tank vehicle;

(5) representing a combination of hazardous materials and tank vehicle endorsements; or

(6) authorizing the driving of a school bus, as defined by Section 541.201 (Vehicles) .

(b) The department may issue a commercial learner’s permit with endorsements authorizing the driving of a passenger vehicle, a school bus, or a tank vehicle.

(c) An endorsement under Subsection (b) for a passenger vehicle or a school bus allows a permit holder to operate a vehicle with only the following passengers:

(1) federal or state auditors and inspectors, test examiners, or other permit holders; and

(2) the commercial driver’s license holder required under Section 522.011 (License or Permit Required; Offense) (a)(2)(B).

(d) An endorsement under Subsection (b) for a tank vehicle allows a permit holder to operate only an empty tank vehicle that has been purged of any hazardous materials.

(e) The holder of a commercial driver’s license or commercial learner’s permit may not drive a vehicle that requires an endorsement unless the proper endorsement appears on the license or permit.

(f) A person commits an offense if the person violates Subsection (c), (d), or (e). An offense under this section is a Class C misdemeanor.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 522. Commercial Driver's Licenses
##### § 522.042 — Endorsements; Offense

(a) The department may issue a commercial driver’s license with endorsements:

    (1) authorizing the driving of a vehicle transporting hazardous materials, subject to the requirements of Title 49 C.F.R. Part 1572;

    (2) authorizing the towing of a double or triple trailer or a trailer over a specified weight;

    (3) authorizing the driving of a vehicle carrying passengers;

    (4) authorizing the driving of a tank vehicle;

    (5) representing a combination of hazardous materials and tank vehicle endorsements; or

    (6) authorizing the driving of a school bus, as defined by Section 541.201 (Vehicles) .

(b) The department may issue a commercial learner’s permit with endorsements authorizing the driving of a passenger vehicle, a school bus, or a tank vehicle.

(c) An endorsement under Subsection (b) for a passenger vehicle or a school bus allows a permit holder to operate a vehicle with only the following passengers:

    (1) federal or state auditors and inspectors, test examiners, or other permit holders; and

    (2) the commercial driver’s license holder required under Section 522.011 (License or Permit Required; Offense) (a)(2)(B).

(d) An endorsement under Subsection (b) for a tank vehicle allows a permit holder to operate only an empty tank vehicle that has been purged of any hazardous materials.

(e) The holder of a commercial driver’s license or commercial learner’s permit may not drive a vehicle that requires an endorsement unless the proper endorsement appears on the license or permit.

(f) A person commits an offense if the person violates Subsection (c), (d), or (e). An offense under this section is a Class C misdemeanor.

*Acts 2015, 84th Leg., R.S., Ch. 752 (H.B.1888), Sec. 21, eff. January 1, 2016.*

authorizing the driving of a vehicle transporting hazardous materials, subject to the requirements of Title 49 C.F.R. Part 1572;
authorizing the towing of a double or triple trailer or a trailer over a specified weight;
authorizing the driving of a vehicle carrying passengers;
authorizing the driving of a tank vehicle;
representing a combination of hazardous materials and tank vehicle endorsements; or
authorizing the driving of a school bus, as defined by Section 541.201 (Vehicles) .

federal or state auditors and inspectors, test examiners, or other permit holders; and
the commercial driver’s license holder required under Section 522.011 (License or Permit Required; Offense) (a)(2)(B).

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: An endorsement under Subsection (b) for a tank vehicle allows a permit holder to operate only an empty tank vehicle that has been purged of any hazardous materials.

subsections.label: e
subsections.text: The holder of a commercial driver’s license or commercial learner’s permit may not drive a vehicle that requires an endorsement unless the proper endorsement appears on the license or permit.

subsections.label: f
subsections.text: A person commits an offense if the person violates Subsection (c), (d), or (e). An offense under this section is a Class C misdemeanor.
